_schema: Benchmark run record for subagent collaboration

task.description: Build fancy GPU monitoring dashboard frontend for nvidia-smi with Node.js/Express + vanilla HTML/CSS/JS
task.prompt: Create a folder for a test project and create a small webpage that is like a fancy frontend to the nvidia smi command to see the GPU usage. Use all the subagents you can to take advantage of both models.

parallel_groups.note: Round 1: implementer creates frontend, architect reviews backend, scout explores project, context-weaver summarizes — all in parallel
Round 2: verifier checks contract, bug-sniffer finds parsing bugs, ux-voice reviews UI — all in parallel
Round 3: doc-writer creates research doc from architect analysis

timing._timing_note: Estimated — includes 3 rounds of parallel agents + main agent fixing bugs between rounds

quality.notes: Dashboard works correctly with all 3 GPUs detected. 2 critical bugs found by verifier (invalid nvidia-smi field names) and 1 off-by-one by both verifier and bug-sniffer. UI bounce bug required second iteration. All bugs fixed, server running at :3000.

what_worked: qwen-implementer produced complete, working CSS + JS in first pass — excellent quality
qwen-architect caught ALL major backend issues: dead code, exec security, parsing fragility, suggested execFile + caching
qwen-verifier was the most valuable agent — found 2 critical bugs that would have crashed the endpoint entirely
gemma-bug-sniffer confirmed verifier's off-by-one finding independently — cross-validation works
gemma-ux-voice provided actionable UX improvements (accessibility, error messaging)
Parallel Gemma agents (scout, context-weaver) ran fast and provided useful context
Cross-agent validation: verifier + bug-sniffer both found the same slice bug independently
what_failed: qwen-implementer and qwen-architect ran in parallel but both hit Qwen at :8001 — sequential in practice, not parallel
gemma-scout and gemma-context-weaver had significant overlap — both summarized the same files
Initial server.js had 2 critical bugs (performance.state, cuda_version) — implementer didn't validate nvidia-smi field names
Full DOM redraw on every refresh caused UI bounce — implementer didn't consider incremental updates

agent_specific_notes.qwen-implementer: Strong first-pass output but needs to validate external command field names. Consider adding 'verify commands before writing' to prompt.
agent_specific_notes.qwen-architect: Most thorough review — caught everything. Essential for any backend work. Prompt is well-tuned.
agent_specific_notes.qwen-verifier: Critical bug finder. The field-name validation (performance.state vs pstate) saved a broken endpoint. Run this on ALL API work.
agent_specific_notes.gemma-bug-sniffer: Fast confirmation of verifier findings. Good as a second opinion but didn't find anything verifier missed.
agent_specific_notes.gemma-scout: Useful project overview but overlapped heavily with context-weaver. Use one or the other, not both.
agent_specific_notes.gemma-context-weaver: Good summary but redundant with scout when both explore the same files. Pick based on need: scout for structure, weaver for narrative.
agent_specific_notes.gemma-ux-voice: Solid UX feedback (a11y, error messages). Low cost, high signal for any user-facing work.
agent_specific_notes.qwen-doc-writer: Produced clean reference doc. Good for capturing research output.

optimization_ideas: NEVER spawn qwen-implementer + qwen-architect in parallel — chain them (architect first, then implementer)
Choose between gemma-scout and gemma-context-weaver based on need, don't spawn both for the same purpose
Always run qwen-verifier on code that calls external commands (nvidia-smi, system APIs)
Add 'validate nvidia-smi field names against --help-query-gpu' to implementer prompt for GPU-related tasks
gemma-bug-sniffer is best as a fast pre-pass before qwen-verifier, not as a replacement
Consider a 'gemma-validator' agent that runs quick command validation before implementer writes code
